parts.DRÁŽNÍ VOZIDLA A URČENÁ TECHNICKÁ ZAŘÍZE: RAILWAY VEHICLES AND DESIGNATED TECHNICAL EQUIPMENT

p1.heading: Subject matter
p1.text:
(1) This Act regulates

- a) conditions for the construction of railway, tramway, trolleybus and cableway tracks and structures on these tracks,

- b) conditions for the operation of tracks pursuant to letter a), for the operation of railway transport on these tracks, as well as the rights and obligations of natural and legal persons associated therewith,

- c) the performance of state administration and state supervision in matters of railway, tramway, trolleybus and cableway tracks.

(2) The Act does not apply to mining, industrial and portable tracks and to ski lifts.

p2.heading: Basic concepts
p2.text:
(1) A track is a path designated for the movement of railway vehicles, including fixed equipment necessary to ensure the safety and smooth flow of railway transport.

(2) The operational capability of a track is the technical condition of the track guaranteeing its safe and smooth operation.

(3) The operation of a track comprises activities that secure and service the track and organize railway transport.

(4) The operation of railway transport is an activity in which a legal relationship arises between the operator of this transport (hereinafter referred to as the „carrier“) and the person whose transport need is satisfied, the subject of which is the transport of persons or goods, or an activity that ensures business in accordance with special regulations.^1)

(5) A tariff is a price list for individual transport services provided and the conditions for their use.

p3.heading: Categories of railway tracks
p3.text:
(1) Railway tracks are divided into individual categories according to their significance, purpose and technical conditions, stipulated by an implementing regulation. The categories of railway tracks are:

- a) national track, which is a track that serves international and national public railway transport and is designated as such,

- b) regional track, which is a track of local significance that serves public railway transport and is connected to a national or other regional track,

- c) siding, which is a track that serves the own needs of the operator or another entrepreneur and is connected to a national or regional track, or another siding,

- d) special track, which serves to ensure the transport accessibility of a municipality.

(2) The railway administrative authority decides on the classification of a railway track into the relevant track category and on changes to this classification.

p4.heading: Railway area
p4.text:
(1) The railway area is the territory designated by a zoning decision for the placement of a railway structure.

(2) The railway area for a national track and a regional track is defined by vertical planes running along the boundaries of the plots of land designated for the placement of the track and its maintenance.

(3) The railway area for other tracks is defined by vertical planes running 3 m from the axis of the outermost track, the outermost supporting or transport cable, the outermost traction line conductor, or the boundaries of the plot of land designated for the placement of the track and its maintenance, but at least 1.5 m from the outer edge of the railway structure, unless the railway transport route is led along a road.

p5.heading: Railway structure and structure on a railway
p5.text:
(1) A railway structure is the construction of a path designated for the movement of railway vehicles and a structure that extends, supplements, changes or secures the track, regardless of whether it is within the railway area or not.

(2) A structure on a railway comprises all structures within the railway area that are not railway structures, regardless of the purpose they serve.

(3) A railway structure and a structure on a railway must meet the technical conditions^2) and safety requirements for the operation of the railway and railway transport. The technical conditions and requirements for individual types of tracks are stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(4) A track as a path designated for the movement of railway vehicles may be cancelled only upon the owner's proposal. The railway administrative authority decides on the cancellation of the track.

p6.heading: Railway crossing
p6.text:
(1) If a railway track crosses roads at track level, the crossing must be marked and secured. The method of marking and securing the crossing is stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) At railway crossings with roads at track level, railway transport has priority over traffic on roads.

p7.heading: Building proceedings
p7.text:
(1) The special building authority^3) for railway structures and structures on a railway is the railway administrative authority.

(2) The suitability of a railway structure for use must be verified by a technical-safety test and trial operation before the issuance of the occupancy permit. The scope and conditions of the technical-safety test and trial operation are stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(3) A structure that is not a railway structure and partially extends into its area may be established and operated only with the consent of the railway administrative authority and under the conditions stipulated by it.

Railway protection zone

p8.text:
(1) The railway protection zone consists of the area on both sides of the track, the boundaries of which are defined by a vertical plane running

- a) for national tracks and regional tracks, 60 m from the axis of the outermost track, but at least 30 m from the boundaries of the railway area,

- b) for national tracks built for speeds greater than 160 km/h, 100 m from the axis of the outermost track, but at least 30 m from the boundaries of the railway area,

- c) for sidings, 30 m from the axis of the outermost track,

- d) for special tracks, 30 m from the boundaries of the railway area; for special track tunnels, 35 m from the axis of the outermost track,

- e) for cableways, 10 m from the supporting cable, transport cable or the axis of the outermost track,

- f) for tramways and trolleybus tracks, 30 m from the axis of the outermost track or the outermost trolley wire.

(2) No protection zone is established for tracks led along roads and for sidings within enclosed operational premises or within a port area.

p9.text:
(1) Within the railway protection zone, structures may be erected and operated, mining activities and activities carried out by mining methods may be performed, shooting ranges may be operated, explosives and hazardous waste may be stored, and light sources and colored surfaces interchangeable with signal signs may be established only with the consent of the railway administrative authority and under conditions stipulated by it.

(2) The railway operator and carrier are authorized to enter third-party land, or structures located thereon, within the railway protection zone for the purpose of repairs, maintenance, and operation of the railway, removal of consequences of accidents or damage to the railway, and for the purpose of removing other obstacles limiting railway transport operations. In doing so, they are obliged to ensure that the use of the land, or structures located thereon, is disturbed as little as possible and that no preventable damage arises from the entry and activities. The exercise of these authorizations must be limited to the necessary time and extent. This provision does not affect the right to compensation for damage under the Civil Code.

(3) The railway operator and carrier are authorized, in a state of emergency or in an urgent public interest in the operation of the railway or railway transport, to use the owner's immovable property within the railway protection zone for the necessary time, to the necessary extent, and for compensation, if the purpose cannot be achieved otherwise.

(4) To ensure the operation of tram and trolleybus lines, the right of use of the owner of adjacent immovable property may be restricted to the necessary extent on the basis of a special law.^4)

p10.heading: Source of railway threat
p10.text: The railway administrative authority identifies sources of threatening, damaging, or disrupting the operation of railways, railway transport, railway telecommunication facilities and lines, and railway safety devices (hereinafter referred to as "source of threat"). Upon identifying a source of threat, it shall order the operator or owner of the source of threat to remove it. If the operator or owner of the source of threat fails to comply, the railway administrative authority shall decide on the removal of the source of threat at their expense. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect.

p11.text:
(1) A railway may be operated by a legal or natural person on the basis of an official permit, unless a special law provides otherwise.^5)

(2) The official permit is issued by the railway administrative authority.

p12.text:
(1) An official permit may be issued on condition that:

- a) the natural person and their responsible representative, if appointed, have reached the age of 21, are legally competent, of good character, and professionally qualified; the applicant does not have to meet the condition of professional qualification if their responsible representative meets it,

- b) the members of the statutory body of the legal person have reached the age of 21, are legally competent, of good character, and at least one member of the statutory body is professionally qualified.

(2) The legal person is obliged to certify the facts stated in paragraph 1 letter b) within 30 days in case of personnel changes in the statutory body.

p13.text: A person shall not be considered of good character under § 12 if they have been finally convicted of a criminal offense committed through negligence for an act related to the permitted activity, or for a criminal offense committed intentionally.

p14.text: The applicant proves professional qualification by a document of completed university education in economics, transport, technical, or legal fields and a document of three years of experience in the field of railway operation, or by a document of completed full secondary vocational education in economics, transport, or technical fields and a document of five years of experience in the field of railway operation.

p15.text:
(1) An application for an official permit must contain:

- a) the business name, registered office, and legal form of the legal person intending to operate the railway, and the name and residence of the person or persons who are its statutory body, and the identification number, if assigned, or the name and permanent residence, business name, and birth number of the natural person intending to operate the railway, as well as the same data concerning the responsible representative, if appointed,

- b) the designation of the railway owner,

- c) the designation of the railway and its description,

- d) data on the time scope of railway operation.

(2) The application for an official permit must be accompanied by:

- a) an extract from the criminal register of the natural person and an extract from the criminal register of the responsible representative, if appointed, if the applicant is a natural person, or an extract from the criminal register of all members of the statutory body, if the applicant is a legal person,

- b) an extract from the commercial register, if it is already a registered legal person, if the applicant is a legal person,

- c) a written document on the existence of a contractual relationship between the applicant and the responsible representative, if appointed, if the applicant is a natural person,

- d) a document proving the professional qualification of the natural person or a document proving the professional qualification of the responsible representative, if appointed, if the applicant is a natural person, or a document proving the professional qualification of at least one of the members of the statutory body, if the applicant is a legal person,

- e) a document certifying the applicant's legal relationship to the railway,

- f) a document proving the technical capability of the railway for its operation,

- g) a document on payment of the administrative fee.

p16.text:
(1) The railway administrative authority shall decide on the issuance of an official permit within 60 days from the delivery of the application for an official permit.

(2) In the decision on the issuance of an official permit, the railway administrative authority shall stipulate conditions for railway operation concerning the assurance of proper and safe railway operation, particularly the condition for the railway operator to issue, by the date of commencement of railway operation, internal regulations on the organization of the railway and railway transport, on the professional qualification of employees ensuring railway operation and organizing railway transport, and to ensure their observance.

(3) The official permit is non-transferable.

p17.text:
In the decision on the issuance of an official permit, the railway administrative authority shall state:

- a) the business name, including the legal form, registered office and identification number, if already assigned, the name and surname of the members of the statutory body, if it is a legal entity, or the name and surname of the railway operator, the business name, birth number, and permanent residence; if an authorized representative is appointed, also information concerning that person, if it is a natural person,

- b) the owner of the railway,

- c) the designation of the operated railway and its description,

- d) the time scope of railway operation,

- e) the date of commencement of railway operation,

- f) the period for which the official permit is issued,

- g) other conditions for railway operation.

p18.heading: Termination of the official permit
p18.text:
(1) If the railway operator does not wish to operate the railway, they are obliged to notify the relevant railway administrative authority of this fact within one year before submitting a proposal for the cancellation of the official permit for railway operation. At the same time, they are also obliged to notify the railway owner within the same period.

(2) The official permit for railway operation shall terminate

- a) by a decision of the railway administrative authority on the cancellation of the official permit, if the railway operator

- 1. ceased to meet the prerequisites for the issuance of the official permit,

- 2. violated the provisions of this Act,

- 3. submitted a proposal for the cancellation of the official permit for railway operation,

- b) upon the expiry of the period for which the official permit was issued,

- c) on the day of the dissolution of the legal entity holding the official permit,

- d) upon the expiry of 30 days from the death of the natural person holding the official permit.

p19.heading: Restriction of railway operation
p19.text:
(1) The railway administrative authority shall decide on the restriction of the operation of a railway or its part based on the request of the railway operator due to the reconstruction of the railway or its part.

(2) The railway operator is entitled to restrict the operation of a railway or its part due to a disruption of the operational capability of the railway or its part as a result of a natural disaster, operational accident or incident, for the period necessary to remove obstacles to the operational capability of the railway.

p20.heading: Obligations of the railway owner
p20.text:
(1) The railway owner is obliged to maintain the railway in such a way as to ensure its operational capability and enable connection with other railways.

(2) If the owner of a national or regional railway cannot ensure its operational capability, they are obliged to offer the railway to the state for purchase. The state is represented by the Ministry of Transport.

(3) The technical conditions for the operational capability of the railway and the technical conditions for railway connection shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p21.heading: Obligations of the railway operator
p21.text:
(1) The railway owner is obliged to ensure the operation of the railway. If the owner of a national or regional railway is not simultaneously its operator, they are obliged to enable the operation of the railway by another authorized person.

(2) If the railway owner cannot ensure the operation of the railway, they are obliged to offer the railway for the purpose of ensuring its operation to:

- a) the state represented by the Ministry of Transport, if it is a national or regional railway,

- b) the municipality or municipalities in whose territorial district the railway is located, if it is a tram, trolleybus, special, or cable railway.

p22.text:
(1) The railway operator is obliged to:

- a) operate the railway according to the rules for railway operation and the valid official permit,

- b) ensure that the operation of the railway is carried out by persons who are medically and professionally qualified,

- c) mark areas within the railway premises that are not accessible to the public,

- d) publish timetables and their changes for public railway passenger transport,

- e) designate the stations (stops) they operate with a name; the operator of a national railway and a regional railway is bound by the decision of the railway administrative authority regarding the name of the station.

(2) The rules for railway operation shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p23.text:
The operator of a national railway and a regional railway is, in addition to the obligations listed in § 22, obliged to:

- a) enable a carrier holding a valid license, based on a contract, to operate railway transport on the railway at a price agreed upon according to price regulations,^6)

- b) provide its transport route for the necessary period to another railway operator for detours around an impassable section of the transport route, if this impassability was caused by a natural disaster or accident,

- c) determine the scope of transport services provided for the transport of persons and goods at individual stations and publish it.

p24.text:
(1) Railway transport on a railway may be operated by a legal or natural person based on a valid license, unless a special law stipulates otherwise,^5) and a concluded contract on railway transport operation, if the railway operator and the carrier are not the same person.

(2) The license is granted by the railway administrative authority based on an application.

p25.text:
(1) A license may be granted on condition that:

- a) the natural person and their authorized representative, if appointed, have reached the age of 18, are legally competent, of good character, and professionally qualified; the applicant does not have to meet the condition of professional qualification if their authorized representative meets it,

- b) the members of the statutory body, if the applicant is a legal entity, have reached the age of 18, are legally competent, are of good character, and at least one member of the statutory body is professionally qualified.

(2) The legal entity is obliged to certify the facts stated in paragraph 1 letter b) within 30 days in case of personnel changes in the statutory body.

p26.text: A person who has been finally convicted of a criminal offense committed through negligence for an act related to the granted license, or for an intentionally committed criminal offense, shall not be considered of good character according to § 25.

p27.text: Professional qualification is demonstrated by the applicant with a document of completed university education in economics, transport, technical, or legal fields and a document of three years of practice in the field of railway transport, or a document of completed full secondary vocational education in economics, transport, or technical fields and a document of five years of practice in the field of railway transport.

p28.text:
(1) An application for a license must contain:

- a) the business name, registered office, and legal form of the legal entity intending to operate rail transport, and the name and residence of the person or persons who are its statutory body and its identification number, if assigned, or the name and permanent residence, business name, and birth registration number of the natural person intending to operate rail transport, as well as the same data concerning the responsible representative, if appointed,

- b) data on the railway line on which rail transport is to be operated, and an indication of the sections of the railway line (track section, line route) in which the applicant intends to operate rail transport,

- c) the designation of the railway infrastructure manager,

- d) data relating to the type of transport and the scope of services provided in connection with the transport of persons and goods,

- e) data on the scope and frequency of transport in a specific time period,

- f) data on the number and type of railway vehicles operated,

- g) a proposal for the timetable of the requested connections, if it concerns public rail passenger transport; for tram, trolleybus, and special railway lines, also a proposal for line routes, including stops for boarding and alighting and the designation of the origin and destination stops.

(2) The license application must be supported by:

- a) an extract from the criminal register of the natural person and an extract from the criminal register of the responsible representative, if appointed, if the applicant is a natural person, or an extract from the criminal register of all members of the statutory body, if the applicant is a legal entity,

- b) written proof of the existence of a contractual relationship between the applicant and the responsible representative, if appointed, if the applicant is a natural person,

- c) an extract from the commercial register, if the applicant is a registered legal entity,

- d) a document proving the professional competence of the natural person, or a document proving the professional competence of the responsible representative, if appointed, if the applicant is a natural person, or a document proving the professional competence of at least one of the members of the statutory body, if the applicant is a legal entity,

- e) proof of payment of the administrative fee.

p29.text:
(1) The Railway Administrative Authority shall decide on the granting of a license within 60 days from the delivery of the application for the license. There is no legal entitlement to the granting of a license.

(2) The Railway Administrative Authority shall not grant a license if the traffic capacity of the railway line on which rail transport is to be operated, or the technical conditions of the railway line, do not permit it.

(3) In its decision on the granting of a license, the Railway Administrative Authority shall stipulate the conditions for operating rail transport, concerning in particular the provision of transport services to the area and the safety of rail transport.

p30.text:
In the decision on the license, the Railway Administrative Authority shall state:

- a) the business name, including the legal form, registered office, and identification number, if already assigned, the name and surname of the members of the statutory body, if it is a legal entity, or the name and surname of the carrier, birth registration number, business name, and permanent residence; if a responsible representative is appointed, also data concerning that person, if it is a natural person,

- b) the designation of the railway line (track section, line route) on which the carrier is authorized to operate rail transport, the names of the stations (stops), and the designation of the origin and destination station (stop),

- c) the designation of the railway infrastructure manager,

- d) the type of transport, the scope of services provided, and the scope and frequency of transport, including the scope of barrier-free transport for passengers with reduced mobility and orientation,

- e) the date of commencement of rail transport operations,

- f) the period for which the license is granted,

- g) other conditions for operating rail transport.

p31.text:
(1) The carrier and the railway infrastructure manager on whose railway line transport is to be operated are bound by the scope and conditions stipulated in the license decision when concluding a contract for the operation of rail transport on the railway line.

(2) Disputes concerning the conclusion of a contract for the operation of rail transport on the railway line and the exercise of rights and obligations arising from this contract shall be decided by the Railway Administrative Authority. An appeal against a decision of the Railway Administrative Authority shall not have suspensive effect.

p32.text:
(1) The Railway Administrative Authority shall decide on a change to the license:

- a) if it is necessary to comply with obligations arising from an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound and which has been promulgated in the Collection of Laws,

- b) if there has been a change in the data specified in the application for the license,

- c) if required by public interest in satisfying transport needs or the safety of rail transport.

(2) The license holder is obliged to notify the Railway Administrative Authority of all changes concerning the data specified in the license application or the fulfillment of the conditions stipulated in the license within 15 days of the occurrence of such changes.

p33.text:
The Railway Administrative Authority may revoke a license if the license holder:

- a) violates the obligations stipulated by this Act,

- b) fails to fulfill the conditions stipulated in the license,

- c) has requested the revocation of the license.

p34.heading: Carrier's Obligations
p34.text:
The license ceases to be valid:

- a) upon the expiry of the period for which it was granted,

- b) on the date of dissolution of the legal entity that is the license holder,

- c) upon the expiry of 30 days from the death of the natural person who is the license holder,

- d) by a decision of the Railway Administrative Authority to revoke the license.

p35.text:
(1) The carrier is obliged to:

- a) operate rail transport according to the rules for operating rail transport, the valid license, and the contract concluded with the railway infrastructure manager for the operation of rail transport on the railway line,

- b) use railway vehicles with a valid certificate of competence and in a technical condition that corresponds to the approved competence when operating rail transport,

- c) ensure that railway vehicles are driven by persons who have a valid driving competence certificate,

- d) ensure that rail transport is carried out by persons who are medically and professionally competent,

- e) comply with the instructions of the railway infrastructure manager given during the organization of rail transport when operating rail transport.

(2) The rules for operating rail transport shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p37.heading: Transport of persons and goods

p38.heading: Restriction of rail transport

p39.heading: Transport under special conditions
p39.text:
(1) Only under special conditions may the following be transported:

- a) dangerous goods,

- b) corpses,

- c) perishable goods,

- d) live animals,

- e) waste,

- f) weapons and ammunition,

- g) rail vehicles on their own wheels,

- h) items of extraordinary dimensions, unusual weight or special design.

(2) The special conditions under which the items listed in paragraph 1 may be transported shall be stipulated by the transport regulations.

Timetable

p40.text: When preparing the timetable for public rail passenger transport on a national railway line and on a regional railway line, the railway infrastructure manager shall base it on the requirements of the carriers contained in the licence application. The railway infrastructure manager shall discuss the draft timetable and the draft of its changes with the district authority through whose territorial district the railway line passes, before its publication.

p41.text:
(1) A carrier who, according to a valid licence, is to operate public rail passenger transport on a tramway line or on a trolleybus line or on a special railway line or on a funicular railway line, shall submit the timetable for approval to the railway administrative authority before commencing rail transport. The railway administrative authority that decides on the granting of the licence shall be competent to approve the timetable.

(2) General regulations on administrative procedure do not apply to the approval of timetables.

p42.text:
(1) The timetable for a national railway line and its changes shall become effective on the date determined by agreement of the European railway administrations. The timetable for a regional railway line and its changes shall become effective on the 15th day from its publication.

(2) The timetable and its changes for a tramway line, trolleybus line, special railway line and funicular railway line shall become effective no earlier than 24 hours from its publication.

(3) The method of preparation, content and publication of the timetable and its changes shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p43.text:
(1) Only a rail vehicle whose design and technical condition comply with the requirements for the safety of rail transport, operating personnel, transported persons and goods, and whose technical fitness has been demonstrated by conformity with the approved type, may be operated on railway lines. Driving rail vehicles, towed rail vehicles with a speed exceeding 160 km/h on railway lines, rail vehicles on tramway lines, trolleybus lines and funicular railway lines must have their technical fitness, in addition to demonstrated conformity with the approved type, also verified by the railway administrative authority. Based on this verification, the railway administrative authority shall issue a vehicle fitness certificate. The method and conditions for approving technical fitness shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) The manufacturer shall demonstrate conformity with the approved type by a type certificate for each vehicle.

(3) The railway administrative authority shall decide on the approval of the type of a rail vehicle based on the application of a domestic or foreign manufacturer of the rail vehicle.

(4) The railway administrative authority shall approve the type of a rail vehicle if the technical conditions for the rail vehicle stipulated by an implementing regulation or an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound and which has been promulgated in the Collection of Laws are met. The basis for the decision of the railway administrative authority shall be the result of the rail vehicle test. The test shall be arranged by the rail vehicle manufacturer at its own expense with a legal entity designated by the Ministry of Transport.

(5) A change to a rail vehicle, the essence of which is an intervention in the vehicle's design signifying a deviation from the approved type, shall be approved by the railway administrative authority according to the conditions stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p44.text:
(1) Rail vehicles in operation, as specified by the implementing regulation, shall be subject to regular technical inspections, which verify the technical condition of the rail vehicle. The method and conditions for carrying out technical condition inspections of the rail vehicle shall be stipulated by the implementing regulation.

(2) If the railway administrative authority finds that a rail vehicle in operation is in a technical condition that does not guarantee the safety of rail transport or does not have the characteristics of the approved type of rail vehicle, it shall decide on the unsuitability of the rail vehicle for operation.

p45.text:
(1) Only persons holding a valid certificate of competence to drive a railway vehicle (hereinafter referred to as „driving“) may drive a railway vehicle.

(2) The railway administrative authority shall issue a certificate of competence to drive to an applicant who has demonstrated professional competence by examination. An applicant may undergo the examination if they:

- a) have reached the prescribed age and education,

- b) are reliable for driving (§ 46),

- c) have demonstrated their health fitness,

- d) have undergone the prescribed instruction and training.

(3) The conditions for health fitness, age and education, the scope of necessary professional knowledge, the method of conducting instruction and training, and the method of conducting examinations shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(4) The railway administrative authority shall order a re-examination of the professional and health fitness of persons for driving if they show deficiencies during driving that may endanger the safety of railway transport. Based on the results of the re-examination, the railway administrative authority may decide to restrict driving or to prohibit driving. In the event of a decision to prohibit driving, the railway administrative authority shall revoke the person's certificate of competence to drive.

p46.text:
A person is not reliable for driving if:

- a) they have been finally convicted of a criminal offence, the factual basis of which is related to driving, or of another criminal offence committed intentionally, if, in view of the driving and the person of the applicant, there is a concern that they will commit the same or a similar act while driving,

- b) a court has imposed a penalty of prohibition of activity consisting of driving.

p47.text:
(1) Pressure, gas, electrical, lifting, transport technical equipment, equipment for protection against the effects of atmospheric and static electricity, equipment for protection against negative effects of reverse traction currents, containers and swap bodies, which are designed and manufactured for railway operation or railway transport and serve to ensure railway operation or railway transport, are designated technical equipment subject to supervision under this Act.

(2) The conditions for the design, manufacture and operation of designated technical equipment and their concretization shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(3) Before designated technical equipment is put into operation, its operational fitness must be approved. The operational fitness of designated technical equipment is approved by the railway administrative authority by issuing a certificate of competence.

(4) The basis for approving the operational fitness of designated technical equipment is a technical inspection and test, which the manufacturer of the designated technical equipment shall arrange at its own expense with a legal entity designated by the Ministry of Transport.

(5) Only designated technical equipment with a valid certificate of competence may be operated during railway operation and railway transport operation.

p48.text:
(1) Revisions, inspections and tests of designated technical equipment in operation may only be carried out by natural persons who hold a valid certificate of professional competence. The certificate of professional competence is issued by the railway administrative authority. The scope of professional knowledge and the method of verifying this knowledge shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) If the railway administrative authority finds that the designated technical equipment has serious deficiencies, and that its safe operation cannot be guaranteed, it shall withhold the certificate of competence and decide on the unsuitability of the designated technical equipment for operation.

(3) If the railway administrative authority finds that an authorized person carrying out revisions, inspections and tests of designated technical equipment shows deficiencies in their activity that substantially affect the verification of technical fitness and the safety of operation of the designated technical equipment, it shall revoke their certificate of professional competence.

p49.text:
(1) A railway accident is an extraordinary event in railway transport that occurred in connection with the movement of a railway vehicle, resulting in death or injury to a person, significant property damage,^8) or endangerment to the life and health of persons.

(2) The railway infrastructure manager and the railway undertaking are obliged to immediately report an accident and any extraordinary event in railway transport that is not related to the movement of a railway vehicle and causes the same consequence as an accident according to paragraph 1, to the authorities of the Police of the Czech Republic.

(3) The railway infrastructure manager and the railway undertaking are obliged, in the event of an accident, to ensure the release of the part of the railway for the resumption of railway operation and railway transport, unless other circumstances prevent it. In doing so, they are obliged to secure and document the state at the time of the accident.

p50.text:
(1) The railway administrative authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 1,000,000 on

- a) the owner of a national or regional railway who fails to ensure the operational capability of the railway,

- b) the owner of a national or regional railway who fails to ensure and enable the operation of the railway by an authorized railway infrastructure manager,

- c) the railway infrastructure manager of a national or regional railway who unlawfully suspends its operation.

(2) The railway administrative authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 500,000 on a railway infrastructure manager who fails to notify the railway administrative authority and the owner of the railway within one year before submitting a proposal for the cancellation of the official permit for railway operation.

(3) The railway administrative authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 1,000,000 on a railway infrastructure manager who

- a) operates a railway without an official permit for railway operation,

- b) does not operate a railway in accordance with the official permit for railway operation or the rules for railway operation.

(4) The railway administrative authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 1,000,000 on a railway infrastructure manager who

- a) allows a person who is not medically fit to perform activities during railway operation,

- b) uses designated technical equipment without a valid certificate of competence during railway operation,

- c) does not allow a railway undertaking that holds a valid license to operate railway transport on a national railway and on a regional railway.

p51.text:
(1) The railway administrative authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 1,000,000 on a carrier who

- a) operates railway transport without a valid license,

- b) restricts railway transport without a decision of the competent authority,

- c) violates the obligations stipulated for the transport of dangerous goods.

(2) The railway administrative authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 1,000,000 on a carrier who

- a) does not operate railway transport in accordance with the rules for operating railway transport, a valid license, or a contract concluded with the railway operator,

- b) disregards the instructions of the railway operator given during the organization of railway transport.

(3) The railway administrative authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 1,000,000 on a carrier who

- a) allows a person without a valid driving license to operate a railway vehicle,

- b) allows persons who are not medically fit to perform activities during the operation of railway transport,

- c) uses a railway vehicle for the operation of railway transport without a valid certificate of competence or in a technical condition that does not correspond to the approved competence,

- d) uses designated technical equipment for the operation of railway transport without a valid certificate of competence.

(4) The railway administrative authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 1,000,000 on a person who

- a) has not removed the source of threat or disruption to the operation of the railway or railway transport,

- b) has damaged railway equipment or railway lines through their activity,

- c) has violated the transport conditions for the transport of dangerous goods.

p52.text:
(1) A fine may be imposed within one year from the day on which the railway administrative authority learned of the violation of the stated obligations, but no later than five years from the time the violation occurred; in the case of ongoing unauthorized operation of a railway or railway transport, no later than five years from the time the unauthorized activity was still ongoing.

(2) When determining the amount of the fine, the seriousness, significance, and duration of the unlawful act and the extent of the damage caused shall be taken into account.

(3) The railway administrative authority shall also collect fines imposed under § 50 and 51.

(4) Fines imposed and collected under this Act are revenue of the state budget. If a municipality imposes a fine, the fine is revenue of the municipality.

p53.text:
(1) The Railway Authority is established as an administrative authority, a state administration body with its registered office in Prague, which is subordinate to the Ministry of Transport.

(2) The head of the Railway Authority is the director, who is appointed and dismissed by the Minister of Transport; the Minister of Transport also approves the statute of the Railway Authority.

p54.text:
(1) State administration in railway matters is exercised by railway administrative authorities, which are the Ministry of Transport and the Railway Authority.

(2) The powers of railway administrative authorities are also exercised by municipalities to the extent stipulated by this Act.

p55.text: The Railway Authority exercises powers under this Act, with the exception of matters decided by the Ministry of Transport or municipalities.

p56.text:
The Ministry of Transport:

- a) decides on the abolition of a national or regional railway in agreement with the Ministry of Defence,

- b) decides on the change of category of a national railway to another category of railway and on the change of category of a railway other than a national railway to a national railway in agreement with the Ministry of Defence,

- c) is the appellate body in administrative proceedings in matters regulated by this Act against decisions of the Railway Authority and municipalities.

p57.heading: State Supervision
p57.text:
(1) The Capital City of Prague^9) exercises powers under this Act in matters of tram, trolleybus, funicular, and special railways, with the exception of:

- a) approval of the type of railway vehicle,

- b) approval of the competence of the railway vehicle,

- c) approval of the competence of designated technical equipment,

- d) decisions on certificates of professional competence of natural persons for carrying out revisions, inspections, and tests of designated technical equipment,

- e) decisions on the suspension or restriction of the operation of designated technical equipment and on the withdrawal of their certificates of competence.

(2) Other municipalities^10) exercise powers under this Act in matters of tram, trolleybus, funicular, and special railways, with the exception of:

- a) approval of the type of railway vehicle,

- b) approval of the competence of the railway vehicle,

- c) approval of the competence of designated technical equipment,

- d) decisions on certificates of professional competence of natural persons for carrying out revisions, inspections, and tests of designated technical equipment,

- e) decisions on the suspension or restriction of the operation of designated technical equipment and on the withdrawal of their certificates of competence,

- f) the competence of a special building authority,

- g) issuing certificates of competence for operating funicular railways.

(3) The municipality competent to decide under this Act is the municipality in whose territorial district the tram, trolleybus, funicular, or special railway is located. If it concerns a decision on granting a license, the competent municipality is the municipality in whose territorial district the starting stop of the railway transport will be located.

p58.text:
(1) State supervision in railway matters is exercised by authorized employees of the Ministry of Transport and the Railway Authority (hereinafter referred to as „authorized persons“).

(2) In exercising state supervision, authorized persons oversee whether railway operators and carriers comply with the conditions and fulfill the obligations stipulated by this Act, whether they operate the railway according to the official permit, and whether they operate railway transport according to a valid license.

p59.text:
(1) The authorized person is entitled, in connection with the exercise of supervision, to enter all premises related to the activity performed, to inspect documents and records concerning the operation of railways and railway transport. The railway operator and the carrier are obliged to enable the authorized person to exercise the aforementioned entitlements, to transport them free of charge along the railway, and to allow free use of railway communication equipment.

(2) When exercising state supervision, the authorized person is obliged to present proof of authorization.

(3) If the authorized person discovers, during the exercise of supervision, a breach of duties stipulated by this Act, or that the operation of the railway is in conflict with the official permit, or that the operation of railway transport is in conflict with a valid license, they shall call upon the railway operator or the carrier to remedy the deficiencies within a set deadline and shall inform the railway administrative authority thereof.

(4) Railway operators and carriers are obliged to remedy deficiencies found during the exercise of state supervision within the set deadline. The railway operator or carrier shall inform the relevant railway administrative authority in writing of the measures taken to remedy the deficiencies.

p60.text:
(1) The Government of the Czech Republic shall decide, within one year from the effective date of this Act, which part of the national railway, operated by České dráhy pursuant to a special act,^5) is a regional railway. The Ministry of Transport is obliged to publish regional railways in the Commercial Bulletin. General regulations on administrative proceedings do not apply to the decision-making process.

(2) When making a decision pursuant to paragraph 1, the part of the national railway listed in the list of lines according to an international agreement by which the Czech Republic is bound^11) shall not be classified as a regional railway.

(3) The operation of railways and railway transport is not a trade according to the Trade Licensing Act.^12)

p61.text: The rights and obligations of the budgetary organization Railway Authority established by the Ministry of Transport existing on the day of its abolition shall pass, on the effective date of this Act, to the Railway Authority established pursuant to this Act.

p62.text:
(1) Carriers operating railway transport on the basis of a concession are obliged to apply for a license (§ 24) within one year from the effective date of this Act. Until the decision of the railway administrative authority, these carriers shall be considered as carriers to whom a license has been granted pursuant to this Act.

(2) Railway operators operating a railway on the basis of a concession are obliged to apply to the railway administrative authority for an official permit for railway operation (§ 11) within one year from the effective date of this Act. Until the decision of the railway administrative authority, these railway operators shall be considered as railway operators to whom an official permit has been issued pursuant to this Act.

(3) Persons who drive a railway vehicle are obliged to apply to the railway administrative authority for a certificate of competence to drive (§ 45) within one year from the effective date of this Act. Until the decision on competence to drive, these persons shall be considered as persons competent to drive pursuant to this Act.

(4) If one natural or legal person is simultaneously a railway operator and a railway transport operator, the keeping of separate accounts for the operation of the railway transport infrastructure and the performance of transport operations is binding for this natural or legal person.

p63.text: Legal relations established by a siding agreement pursuant to Ministry of Transport Decree No. 126/1964 Sb., on contact between national railways and sidings and on relations between participating organizations (siding conditions), which arose before the effective date of this Act, as well as rights arising therefrom, shall be governed by the existing regulations.

p64.heading: Proceedings in matters regulated by this Act
p64.text: Proceedings in matters regulated by this Act shall be conducted in accordance with general regulations on administrative proceedings, unless individual provisions of this Act stipulate otherwise.

p65.text: The transport regulations governing transport conditions on national railways and regional railways may deviate from the provisions of the Commercial Code, if they adopt provisions of international treaties by which the Czech Republic is bound and which have been promulgated in the Collection of Laws.

p66.heading: Authorizing provisions
p66.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport shall issue legal regulations for the implementation of § 3 para. 1, § 5 para. 3, § 6 para. 1, § 7 para. 2, § 20 para. 3, § 22 para. 2, § 35 para. 2, § 42 para. 3, § 43 para. 1, § 43 para. 4, § 43 para. 5, § 44 para. 1, § 45 para. 3, § 47 para. 2 and § 48 para. 1.

(2) The Ministry of Transport, in agreement with the Ministry of Health, shall issue a legal regulation on the conditions of health fitness of persons for the operation of railways and railway transport and on the method of performing assessment activities.

(3) The Ministry of Transport, in agreement with the Ministry of Interior, shall issue a legal regulation on the conduct of instruction, training and examinations of applicants for a certificate of competence to drive trams and trolleybuses.

(4) The Ministry of Transport shall issue a legal regulation specifying the components of a railway.

p67.heading: Repealing provisions
p67.text:
The following are hereby repealed:

- 1. Act No. 51/1964 Sb., on Railways, as amended by Act No. 104/1974 Sb.,

- 2. Ministry of Transport Decree No. 52/1964 Sb., implementing the Act on Railways, as amended by Decrees No. 132/1969 Sb. and No. 122/1974 Sb.,

- 3. Ministry of Transport Decree No. 68/1964 Sb., on the transfer of powers of state administration in railway matters, as amended by Decree No. 11/1979 Sb., on the powers of national railway administrations in the field of state specialized technical supervision of railways, and Decree No. 177/1988 Sb.,

- 4. Ministry of Transport Decree No. 126/1964 Sb., on the interface between national railways and sidings and on relations between participating organizations (siding conditions), as amended by Decrees No. 111/1966 Sb., No. 2/1980 Sb., No. 42/1984 Sb. and No. 74/1985 Sb.,

- 5. Federal Ministry of Transport Decree No. 11/1979 Sb., on the powers of national railway administrations in the field of state specialized technical supervision of railways,

- 6. Federal Ministry of Transport Decree No. 123/1979 Sb., on urban public transport,

- 7. Federal Ministry of Transport Decree No. 18/1981 Sb., on the approval of railway vehicles and special railway mechanization equipment,

- 8. Federal Ministry of Transport Decree No. 61/1982 Sb., on designated technical equipment,

- 9. Federal Ministry of Transport Decree No. 72/1984 Sb., on the conditions for the suitability of ISO series 1 containers for safe and smooth operation,

- 10. Federal Ministry of Transport Decree No. 8/1987 Sb., on the suitability of imported cableways and other technical equipment,

- 11. Federal Ministry of Transport Decree No. 7/1988 Sb., on the crossing of land communications with railways at track level,

- 12. Federal Ministry of Transport Decree No. 190/1988 Sb., which sets fixed amounts of compensation for causing minor disruptions in train operation on national railways and sidings, as amended by Decree No. 316/1992 Sb.,

- 13. Federal Ministry of Transport Decree No. 38/1990 Sb., which sets certain conditions for ensuring the safe and smooth operation of lifting equipment for handling ISO series 1 containers,

- 14. Directive of the Federal Ministry of Transport, the General Prosecutor's Office of the Czechoslovak Socialist Republic and the Federal Ministry of the Interior Ref. No. 11471/1973 on the coordination and cooperation of transport, security and prosecution authorities in railway accidents (registered in Part 12/1973 Sb.),

- 15. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 11 446/1974 on the regular repeated re-examination of crane operators and slingers working with road boom cranes or railway track boom cranes (registered in Part 15/1974 Sb.),

- 16. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 19 239/1974 on the deadlines for supervisory activity of state specialized technical supervision (registered in Part 15/1974 Sb.),

- 17. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 12 142/1975-18 on the lifting equipment of the upper platform of a double-deck railway car for the transport of passenger cars, as amended by Edict Ref. No. 23 229/79-025 (registered in Part 33/1975 Sb. and Part 6/1980 Sb.),

- 18. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 12 241/75-025 on the powers of national railway administrations as railway administrative bodies, as amended by Edict Ref. No. 25 112/77 (registered in Part 2/1973 Sb. and in Part 20/1975 Sb.),

- 19. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport and the Czech Mining Authority No. 3939/19/1976 on the rules of technical operation for mine railways of surface brown coal mines (PTP-D) (registered in Part 4/1979 Sb.),

- 20. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport No. 26 049/76, issuing the Examination Regulations for drivers, conductors, dispatchers, stationmasters and transport control personnel of urban public transport, as amended by Edict No. 7 129/1982-025 (registered in Part 8/1977 Sb. and in Part 9/1982 Sb.),

- 21. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport No. 19 022/1978 on professional competence in electrical engineering on railways (registered in Part 32/1978 Sb.),

- 22. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 8 624/1982-25, which sets fixed amounts of compensation for damage to railway cars and equipment, as amended by Edict Ref. No. 8 682/1992-040 (registered in Part 16/1982 Sb. and in Part 81/1992 Sb.),

- 23. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 9 737/1984 on the transport of military container shipments (registered in Part 22/1984 Sb.),

- 24. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 5 095/85-ÚÚŽZ, issuing directives on the health fitness of railway workers (registered in Part 16/1985 Sb.),

- 25. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 8 969/85, which sets certain conditions for the performance of state specialized technical supervision to ensure the safety of ISO series 1 containers in operation (registered in Part 24/1985 Sb.).

p68.text:
This Act shall enter into force on 1 January 1995.

**Uhde m.p.**

**Havel m.p.**

**Klaus m.p.**

^3) § 120 of Act No. 50/1976 Sb., on territorial planning and the Building Code (the Building Act), as amended.

^4) E.g., § 128 of Act No. 40/1964 Sb., the Civil Code, as amended.

^5) Act No. 9/1993 Sb., on Czech Railways, as amended by Act No. 212/1993 Sb.

^6) Act No. 526/1990 Sb., on prices.

^7) E.g., Act No. 238/1991 Sb., on waste. Decree No. 72/1988 Sb., on the use of explosives, as amended. Annex I to Decree No. 8/1985 Sb., on the Convention concerning International Carriage by Rail (COTIF).

^8) Act No. 140/1961 Sb., the Criminal Code, as amended.

^9) § 13 and 26 of Czech National Council Act No. 418/1990 Sb., on the Capital City of Prague, as amended.

^10) § 58 (5) of Czech National Council Act No. 367/1990 Sb., on municipalities (municipal establishment), as amended.

^11) European Agreement on Main International Railway Lines (AGC Agreement), approved by Resolution of the Government of the Czechoslovak Socialist Republic No. 78 of 8 February 1990.

^12) Act No. 455/1991 Sb., on self-employment (the Trades Licensing Act), as amended.
